title: Spot a Fake Review
summary: Identify signs that an online review might be fake or paid.
learnerPromise: You will be able to spot warning signs that suggest a review is fake or paid for, helping you trust reviews more wisely.
whyItMatters: Not all online reviews are honest. Some are paid for or written by people who never used the product. Learning to spot fake reviews helps you avoid being misled.
realLifeUses: Noticing that several 5-star reviews use the exact same phrases
Spotting a review that describes features the product does not actually have
Recognizing when a reviewer has only ever reviewed products from one brand
Seeing a burst of positive reviews posted within a few days of each other
result: A checklist of red flags you can use to judge review trustworthiness.
youWillLearn: Common warning signs in fake reviews like vague praise or copied phrases
How to check a reviewer's history for patterns
How to compare reviews across different websites
How to balance positive and negative feedback for a fairer picture
miniChallenge: Find a product with many reviews. Look through them and write down at least 3 signs that a review might be fake or unreliable.
steps: Read a mix of positive and negative reviews for one product.
Look for vague language: reviews that say 'great product' without any details.
Check if multiple reviews use very similar wording or were posted on the same day.
Click on a reviewer's profile to see if they only review one brand or product type.
Compare the same product's reviews on another website to see if the feedback is consistent.
completionCriteria: You identified at least 3 red flags that suggest a review may be fake
You checked reviewer profiles or cross-site reviews for patterns
You can explain why a specific review seems trustworthy or not
commonProblems: Fake reviews can look very real — look for patterns, not just one clue
Some negative reviews are fake too, posted by competitors
Review filters on shopping sites are not perfect — use your own judgment
tips: Reviews with specific details and photos tend to be more reliable
A mix of positive and negative points in one review is often more honest
If almost every review is 5 stars, be cautious — most products have at least some variation
